title: Taco Tuesday Open Source Builder Nights: OpenClaw Demo

venue.name: Zelifcam
venue.address: 10200 San Pedro Ave, San Antonio, TX, 78216

description:
San Antonio's tech community has been busy all month, and we're closing out AI April with a collaboration between Alamo Tech Collective and Geeks && {...} at The Hackerspace. This special edition of our monthly Taco Tuesday Builder Night zeroes in on how AI tools are reshaping how we code, ship, and handle our daily work. Our guest speaker, Patrick Robinson, is back to demo OpenClaw, walking through how it fits into workflows and where it can genuinely save you time. Bring a laptop, a project you're poking at, and an appetite. We'll have tacos, open build time, spontaneous pairing, and a room full of SA devs and enthusiasts. This is also the perfect warm-up for the 2nd Annual AI April Showcase on May 2nd, a science-fair-style event where builders of all levels show off AI-powered projects, and attendees vote for the People's Choice Award. If you're already prepping a demo or just want to see what's possible, Builder Night is a great place to spark (or sharpen) what you bring. Heads up: we're also collecting non-perishable items for the San Antonio Food Bank, so toss something in the red bin on your way in. Whether you're AI-curious, AI-skeptical, or already shipping with it, come build with us and cap off AI April the ATC way.

This session is all about collaboration, progress, and shared problem-solving. Come with a project in mind or jump in with someone else’s.

What to expect:

* Quick kickoff and space to share what you’re working on
* Demos or updates for those who want to show progress
* Open working time with a room full of builders
* Casual conversation, spontaneous pairing, and idea swaps
* Tacos. Because why not.

Bring your laptop, your curiosity, and your appetite.

title: Byte Night

venue.name: Zelifcam
venue.address: 10200 San Pedro Ave, San Antonio, TX, 78216

description:
Byte Night is a laid-back gathering for programmers, tech folks, indie hackers, and curious minds in San Antonio who just want to hang out, talk shop, share ideas, and code together.

No pressure, no pitches — just good conversations about programming, business, tech trends, side projects, and whatever else comes up. Bring your laptop if you want to hack on something, or just come for the company and the chatter.

Whether you're building the next big thing, tinkering with personal projects, or just looking to meet other people who *get* it, you’re welcome here.

✨ **What to expect:**

* Chill vibes and great conversations
* People working on cool stuff (or nothing at all)
* Tech talk, idea-sharing, and occasional brainstorming
* Everyone from seasoned devs to beginners welcome
* Probably tacos or coffee involved (this is San Antonio, after all)

**Come hang out. Let’s talk code, life, and everything in between.**
**We have a donation area for the San Antonio Food Bank! Don't forget to bring non-perishable items to help out.**
